outcomes.change_description: Revise to distinguish between clearly regulated insoluble acrylate microparticles and contested carbomer/soluble polymer status. Add explicit caveat that ECHA guidance on water-soluble polymer exemptions is actively under review by Cosmetics Europe and ECHA, and that carbomer applicability is unresolved as of 2025-2026.

Cap confidence at 0.45 pending revision.

If 12x upper bound is to be retained, provide explicit sourced comparison against lowest-cost commodity polyolefins (e.g., HDPE at ~$0.5/kg) as a separate data point.

outcomes.node_label: Spider Silk Cosmetics ScienceDirect Review 2025
Keratin Hydrolysate (Fermentation-Derived): MW-Dependent Film vs Penetration Dual Function
outcomes.challenge_summary: Revise title from 'Fermentation-Derived: Keratin Hydrolysate' to 'Hydrolyzed Keratin: Animal-Byproduct Enzyme Hydrolysate'. Remove 'fermentation-derived' throughout. Add explicit note that keratin hyd
outcomes.change_description: Revise title from 'Fermentation-Derived: Keratin Hydrolysate' to 'Hydrolyzed Keratin: Animal-Byproduct Enzyme Hydrolysate'. Remove 'fermentation-derived' throughout. Add explicit note that keratin hydrolysates do NOT qualify as precision fermentation biopolymers — the substrate is animal-derived; keratinase enzyme may be produced by Bacillus fermentation but the product is a hydrolysate. Note COSMOS certification and bio-based polymer classification implications. Lower confidence to 0.35.

outcomes.change_description: Remove the '~65% of industrial HA supply' market share figure or label explicitly as manufacturer-estimated without independent source. outcomes.change_description: Revise to clearly distinguish S. cerevisiae TRL by compound class: 'TRL 5-7 for antioxidants, bioactive peptides, and some moisturizers; HA production in S. cerevisiae is at research stage (TRL 3-4) and not commercially deployed — commercial HA fermentation uses Bacillus and Streptococcus strains at TRL 9.' outcomes.node_label: Geltor Precision Fermentation Collagen Platform
Xanthan vs Carbomer: 10x Higher Use Level Required
outcomes.challenge_summary: Revise framing from 'use-level disadvantage' to 'use-level and texture-profile difference.' Add caveat that viscosity equivalence is shear-rate and measurement-condition dependent, and that xanthan an
outcomes.change_description: Revise framing from 'use-level disadvantage' to 'use-level and texture-profile difference.' Add caveat that viscosity equivalence is shear-rate and measurement-condition dependent, and that xanthan and carbomer produce fundamentally distinct rheological profiles not directly interchangeable at any concentration ratio. Link to [1943c3f03f8b] as supporting context. Retain unsourced flag and 0.50 confidence cap.

outcomes.change_description: Revise to label '4x moisture retention vs HA' as supplier-reported comparative data (Vedan and similar Japanese producers), not independently peer-reviewed across MW grades and standardized measurement conditions. Add caveat that moisture retention advantage is MW-dependent and a single multiplier is an oversimplification. Lower confidence from 0.80 to 0.58.

outcomes.change_description: Correct NATRUE description to: 'NATRUE brand recognition threshold requires ≥75% of a brand's natural/organic product range to be NATRUE-certified before the brand mark may be used holistically; certification is evaluated at the individual product level.' Revise synbio barrier language to specify that NATRUE restricts GMO-engineered production strain products specifically; fermentation biopolymers from non-GMO strains (xanthan, pullulan, non-GMO Bacillus HA) are not categorically excluded.
